meta_title: Impact Doors in Twin Lakes, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Twin Lakes, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, $0 down financing. Protect your home — call today!
h1: Impact Doors Built for Twin Lakes Homes — Security, Style & Hurricane Protection
intro:
Twin Lakes may sit about seven miles inland from the Atlantic shoreline, but that distance offers little comfort when a major hurricane sweeps through Broward County. Residents here know the drill — the boards come out, the shutters go up, and the anxiety sets in. What if there was a permanent solution that protected your home around the clock without the seasonal panic? Impact doors from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing are exactly that solution, offering round-the-clock protection for Twin Lakes homeowners without sacrificing curb appeal or everyday convenience.

The Twin Lakes community is a tight-knit neighborhood anchored by the charm of Twin Lakes Park and the quiet residential streets that make Broward County living so attractive. With average home values sitting around $270,000, homeowners here have made a meaningful investment in their properties — one that deserves meaningful protection. A full 55% of homes in the area were built before 1994, meaning they predate Florida's modern hurricane building codes and were never designed to withstand the punishing wind loads that today's standards require. For these older homes especially, upgrading to impact-rated entry doors, sliding glass doors, or French doors isn't just a smart improvement — it's a critical safety measure.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has completed more than 500 home improvement projects across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and we understand the unique challenges that Twin Lakes and surrounding communities like Lauderhill, Tamarac, and Sunrise present. Older construction, varied permitting timelines, and the need for affordable financing options are all realities we've navigated for years. Our team brings certified expertise, top-tier product lines, and a genuine commitment to service that has earned us an A+ BBB rating and the reputation as The Kings of Service throughout South Florida.

section_why.h2: Why Twin Lakes Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Door Installations
section_why.body:
When it comes to upgrading your home with impact doors, the installer matters as much as the product. Too many South Florida homeowners have experienced the frustration of subcontracted crews, missed timelines, and installations that don't hold up under inspection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing operates differently. Every installation we perform is completed entirely by our own in-house team — no subcontractors, no hand-offs, no corners cut. Our installation crews bring over 60 years of combined experience to every project, and that expertise shows in the precision of every frame, every seal, and every multi-point lock we put in place.

For Twin Lakes homeowners, working with a local South Florida company means we understand the specific conditions your home faces. We know the wind patterns that funnel through central Broward County, the soil and construction quirks common in pre-1994 builds, and the permit requirements set by the Broward County Building Division. We pull every permit, coordinate every inspection, and handle the paperwork so you don't have to. This is particularly valuable for older Twin Lakes homes where structural considerations may require additional attention during door frame preparation.

Our product offerings for Twin Lakes include impact-resistant entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors from two industry-leading brands: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both lines are engineered to meet or exceed Florida Building Code requirements for Broward County's 170 mph wind zone, and both offer a variety of styles and finishes that complement the residential character of the Twin Lakes neighborhood. Whether you're replacing a weathered front door or finally upgrading that aging sliding glass door that leads to your backyard, we have a product and a team ready to make it happen.

From your initial free estimate to the final walkthrough after installation, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is with you every step of the way. We don't believe in high-pressure sales tactics. We believe in honest assessments, clear pricing, and exceptional workmanship — which is exactly why homeowners throughout Twin Lakes, Lauderhill, Tamarac, and Sunrise continue to refer us to their neighbors.

section_history.h2: Hurricane History Proves Why Twin Lakes Homes Need Upgraded Impact Doors
section_history.body:
Twin Lakes and the surrounding Broward County region have been tested by storms more times than most residents care to count. Since 1990, the area has been impacted by nine named storms, with three standing out as particularly destructive benchmarks in South Florida's hurricane memory. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was a watershed moment — a Category 5 storm that redefined how the state approached residential construction and wind mitigation. Homes in communities like Twin Lakes that survived Andrew often did so by sheer luck, not design. The destruction Andrew left behind throughout South Florida directly prompted the sweeping overhaul of Florida's building codes in 1994.

More than a decade later, Hurricane Wilma in 2005 delivered another sobering reminder of the region's vulnerability. Wilma struck Broward County with sustained winds that caused widespread damage to doors, windows, and roofing systems across central Broward neighborhoods. Many Twin Lakes homeowners experienced firsthand how quickly a compromised entry door or sliding glass door can allow wind and water intrusion to devastate a home's interior. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which swept through the county with enough force to cause significant damage even to some homes that had upgraded their protection after Wilma. The lesson across all three storms is consistent: older doors and standard construction simply aren't enough.

The data is clear — with 55% of Twin Lakes homes built before 1994's post-Andrew code reforms, a large portion of the community is still relying on door systems never designed for modern hurricane standards. Impact doors aren't a luxury upgrade for Twin Lakes residents; they're a practical, permanent layer of defense that eliminates the annual scramble for plywood and shutters while providing superior protection every single day of the year.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code & Broward County Permit Requirements for Impact Doors
section_code.body:
Twin Lakes falls within Broward County's jurisdiction and is governed by the Florida Building Code (FBC), which sets strict requirements for all exterior openings including entry doors, French doors, and sliding glass doors. While Twin Lakes is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation reserved for Miami-Dade County and portions of Broward closest to the coast — the area is still subject to rigorous hurricane-rated product requirements under the FBC. All impact doors installed in Twin Lakes must meet design pressure ratings appropriate for the local 170 mph wind zone, and products must carry valid Florida Product Approval numbers to qualify for legal installation.

Every impact door installation in Twin Lakes requires a permit pulled through the Broward County Building Division. This process involves submitting product specifications, installation drawings, and compliance documentation before work begins. Upon completion, the installation must pass a formal inspection by a county building inspector to confirm code compliance. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles this entire process on behalf of our customers — from application to final sign-off. Many homeowners are surprised to learn that unpermitted door work can create major complications when it's time to sell a home or file an insurance claim, making professional permit management an essential part of any reputable installation.

For pre-1994 homes — which make up more than half of Twin Lakes' residential stock — code compliance can require additional structural reinforcement around door openings to properly anchor impact-rated frames. Our experienced team identifies and addresses these conditions during our initial assessment, ensuring that your completed installation not only looks great but is fully compliant with Broward County standards from day one.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premier-Grade Products for Twin Lakes Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors from two trusted industry brands: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both manufacturers have built their reputations on producing hurricane-impact products engineered for the demanding conditions of South Florida, and both are well-suited to the specific wind zone requirements that Twin Lakes homeowners face. Whether you're looking for a grand statement entry door, a sleek sliding glass door for your patio, or elegant French doors, our product lineup has options that deliver on both performance and aesthetics.

ESW impact doors are engineered with multi-point locking systems that distribute force across the entire door frame rather than concentrating stress at a single latch point — a critical feature during high-wind events. ESW products carry Florida Product Approval ratings for Broward County's 170 mph design pressure requirements, and their laminated glass construction is designed to remain intact even when impacted, preventing the dangerous wind and water intrusion that causes the majority of storm-related interior damage. Their entry doors and sliding glass doors are available in a range of finishes that complement the residential styles common throughout Twin Lakes.

CWI rounds out our product offering with a strong lineup of impact-rated doors that emphasize thermal efficiency alongside storm protection. For Twin Lakes homeowners concerned about year-round energy costs — a legitimate concern in South Florida's relentless heat — CWI's insulated impact door systems provide meaningful improvements in thermal performance. Reduced air infiltration and better insulated frames translate directly into lower cooling bills, making CWI doors a smart investment that pays dividends well beyond hurricane season. Both ESW and CWI products are backed by manufacturer warranties, and every installation we complete is backed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's workmanship guarantee.

section_cost.h2: Impact Door Costs & Flexible Financing Options for Twin Lakes Homeowners
section_cost.body:
For Twin Lakes homeowners with average home values around $270,000, cost is understandably a central consideration when evaluating any major home improvement project. Impact door pricing varies based on the type of door — entry, sliding glass, or French — the size of the opening, the specific product selected, and any structural preparation required. As a general range, homeowners in Twin Lakes can expect impact entry door replacements to start in the mid-hundreds and climb depending on design complexity, while larger sliding glass or French door systems typically represent a higher investment. Our team provides detailed, transparent quotes so you know exactly what to expect before any work begins.

To make impact door upgrades accessible for Twin Lakes families across all income levels, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. This means you can begin protecting your home and family today without waiting until you've saved a lump sum. The return on investment for impact doors in South Florida is among the best of any home improvement category — studies consistently show that impact-rated openings increase resale value, improve buyer confidence, and differentiate your home in a competitive Broward County market.

When you factor in the insurance savings that typically accompany a wind mitigation upgrade (more on that in the next section), the effective monthly cost of impact doors drops further. Many Twin Lakes homeowners find that their annual insurance reduction alone offsets a meaningful portion of their monthly financing payment, making the true out-of-pocket cost far lower than the sticker price suggests. Add in the elimination of annual shutter costs, storage fees, and storm prep labor, and the financial case for impact doors becomes even more compelling.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Twin Lakes Homeowners After Impact Door Installation
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate and tangible financial benefits of installing impact doors in Twin Lakes is the reduction in homeowners insurance premiums. Florida law requires insurance carriers to offer discounts for homes with wind-mitigation features, and impact-rated doors and windows are among the most heavily weighted factors in a wind mitigation inspection report. Once your new impact doors are installed and inspected, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing recommends scheduling a wind mitigation inspection with a licensed inspector — a process that typically takes less than an hour and produces a report you submit directly to your insurance carrier.

The resulting discounts vary by carrier and policy, but Twin Lakes homeowners regularly report annual premium reductions ranging from several hundred to over a thousand dollars when upgrading their homes with impact-rated openings. Common carriers operating throughout Broward County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and others serving the South Florida market — are all required under Florida Statute 627.0629 to apply these credits. Homes that complete a full impact upgrade, including both doors and windows, often see the most dramatic premium reductions, but even a partial upgrade covering doors alone produces meaningful savings.

Beyond the premium discounts, impact doors provide an insurance benefit that's harder to quantify but just as real: the reduced likelihood of a major claim. Wind and water intrusion through compromised doors is one of the leading causes of interior storm damage, and that type of damage can result in claims that raise your rates or even trigger policy cancellation in a Florida insurance market that has grown increasingly restrictive. By making your Twin Lakes home more resilient, you're not just saving money today — you're protecting your insurability for the long term.

faqs.q: Do Twin Lakes homes really need impact doors if they're not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Twin Lakes sits in Broward County's 170 mph wind zone, and being seven miles inland doesn't significantly reduce your exposure during a major hurricane. In fact, storms like Wilma and Irma demonstrated that wind damage and pressure differentials affect inland homes just as severely as coastal ones. With 55% of Twin Lakes homes built before 1994's updated building codes, impact door upgrades are especially important for protecting these older structures from wind and water intrusion.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in Twin Lakes, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Costs vary based on door type, size, and any structural prep needed — but Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available. Given Twin Lakes' average home value of $270,000, impact doors are a high-ROI investment. When you factor in insurance premium savings and the elimination of annual shutter costs, many homeowners find the net cost is significantly lower than expected. Contact us for a free, no-obligation quote tailored to your home.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to install impact doors in Twin Lakes, Broward County?
faqs.a: Yes. All impact door installations in Twin Lakes require a permit through the Broward County Building Division, followed by a formal inspection upon completion. Products must carry valid Florida Product Approval numbers appropriate for the local 170 mph wind zone. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — from application to final inspection sign-off — so you never have to worry about compliance or paperwork. Unpermitted installations can cause major issues during home sales or insurance claims.

faqs.q: How long does the impact door installation process take in Twin Lakes?
faqs.a: Most residential impact door installations in Twin Lakes are completed in one to two days, depending on the number of openings and any structural preparation required. The full timeline from signed contract to completed installation typically ranges from two to four weeks, accounting for permitting, product ordering, and scheduling. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's in-house team coordinates every step, keeping you informed throughout the process so there are no surprises. We work efficiently while maintaining the quality standards our A+ BBB rating reflects.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowners insurance after installing impact doors in Twin Lakes?
faqs.a: Savings vary by carrier and policy, but Twin Lakes homeowners commonly see annual premium reductions ranging from a few hundred dollars to over $1,000 after a wind mitigation inspection confirms their impact-rated doors. Florida law requires carriers to apply these credits under Statute 627.0629. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing recommends scheduling a wind mitigation inspection immediately after installation so you can submit your report and start saving right away. A full impact upgrade covering both doors and windows typically produces the greatest combined premium reduction.
